deploy_framework: vitepress (docs/.vitepress/ — VitePress project; pre-existing docs/.vitepress/dist build output in tree)
pages_workflow: .github/workflows/pages.yml — 'Deploy Dashboard to Pages', ARCHIVED (header '# ARCHIVED — pages deployment disabled'), both jobs guarded with 'if: false', uploads 'dashboard/dist'
fix_recommendation: MISMATCH: pages.yml ships a React+Vite dashboard build from dashboard/ → dashboard/dist, but repo also contains a VitePress site under docs/.vitepress/ with a pre-built docs/.vitepress/dist already on disk. Pages Source is set to path: /docs but no `docs/index.html` exists — `docs/` only contains `.vitepress/` and `node_modules/`. Two valid fixes: (A) If Pages should serve the docs site, un-archive pages.yml, change upload path to `docs/.vitepress/dist`, add a `bunx vitepress build docs` build step, and set Pages Source to 'GitHub Actions'. (B) If Pages should serve the React dashboard, un-archive pages.yml, keep `dashboard/dist` as upload path, set Pages Source to 'GitHub Actions', and either delete the stray docs/ dir or repurpose it. Recommended: option A — the VitePress build output already exists in-tree, making this a no-build-content fix. Remove the two `if: false` guards and the ARCHIVED header.
